heading: Congressional statement of purpose
label: 3601 Congressional statement of purpose
text: It is the purpose of this chapter to provide legislation necessary or desirable for the implementation of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 between the United States of America and the Republic of Panama and of the related agreements accompanying that Treaty.
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , known as the Panama Canal Act of 1979, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note below and Tables.Pub. L. 96–7093 Stat. 452Sept. 27, 1979

Ex. Ord. No. 12173. Continuing Applicability of Panama Canal RegulationsEx. Ord. No. 12173, , 44 F.R. 69271, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12203, , 45 F.R. 20451, provided:Nov. 29, 1979Mar. 26, 1980By the authority vested in me as President of the United States of America by of the United States Code, by the Panama Canal Code (), as amended, and by () [see Short Title note above], and in accordance with the rights granted to the United States of America by the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 “to manage, operate, and maintain the Panama Canal, its complementary works, installations and equipment and to provide for the orderly transit of vessels through the Panama Canal,” it is hereby ordered as follows:Section 301 of Title 376A Stat. 1Public Law 96–7093 Stat. 4521–101. All regulations that were adopted by the President or his delegates pursuant to former Title 2 of the Canal Zone Code (), repealed by Section 303(a)(1) [probably should be 3303(a)(1)] of , or actions taken pursuant thereto, that were in effect on , and that address matters which the President is authorized to regulate pursuant to [see Short Title note above], shall remain in effect unless or until amended, superseded, or otherwise terminated by the President or the Panama Canal Commission. This extension shall not apply to the extent that any such regulation or action is inconsistent with the provisions of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977, its implementing agreements, or .76A Stat. 6–50Public Law 96–70Public Law 96–70Public Law 96–70September 30, 19791–102. The Secretary of Defense shall exercise the powers and carry out the responsibilities vested in the President of the United States by the Panama Canal Code (), as amended, and (), except for those powers and responsibilities vested in the President by Sections 1102(b), 1103, 1104, 1105(a), 1106(b), 1108, 1109(a), 1112(d), 1243(a)(1), 1321(c), 1344(b), 1504(b), 1601(a), 2206(b) and 3301 of [sections 3612(b), 3613, 3614, 3615(a), 3616(b), 3618, 3619(a), 3622(d), 3681(a)(1), 3731(c), 3754(b), 3784(b), 3791(a), [former] 3844(b) and 3871 of this title]. This delegation shall be effective until .76A Stat. 1Public Law 96–7093 Stat. 452Public Law 96–70May 15, 1980Jimmy Carter.

Ex. Ord. No. 12215. Delegation of Panama Canal FunctionsEx. Ord. No. 12215, , 45 F.R. 36043, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12652, , 53 F.R. 36775, provided:May 27, 1980Sept. 19, 1988By the authority vested in me as President of the United States of America by the Panama Canal Code (), as amended, by the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; et seq.), and by of the United States Code, it is hereby ordered as follows:76A Stat. 193 Stat. 45222 U.S.C. 3601Section 301 of Title 3

1–1.The Secretary of Defense1–101. The Secretary of Defense shall develop for the President’s consideration an appropriate legislative proposal as required by Section 3(d) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ). The Secretary of Defense shall coordinate development of this proposal with the Secretary of State and the heads of other interested Executive agencies.93 Stat. 45622 U.S.C. 3602(d)1–102. The function vested in the President by Section 1212(d)(1) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) to exclude employees of, or positions within, the Department of Defense from coverage under any provision of subchapter II, Chapter 2 of Title I of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 [ et seq.], is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 46422 U.S.C. 3652(d)(1)22 U.S.C. 36511–103. The function vested in the President by of the Panama Canal Code (; 6 P.C.C. 1281(b)), as amended, with respect to areas and installations made available to the United States pursuant to the Agreement in Implementation of Article IV of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.Section 1281(b) of Title 676A Stat. 4551–104. The function vested in the President by Section 1701 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ), with respect to regulations applicable within the areas and installations made available to the United States pursuant to the Agreement in Implementation of Article IV of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977, is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 49222 U.S.C. 38011–105. The functions vested in the President by Sections 1243(c)(1) and 2401 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 ( and 495; and 3851) are delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 47422 U.S.C. 3681(c)(1)1–106. The functions vested in the President by Section 1502(a) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) are delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 48822 U.S.C. 3782(a)

1–2.Coordination of Pay and Employment Practices1–201. In order to coordinate the policies and activities of agencies under subchapter II of Chapter 2 of Title I of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; et seq.), each agency shall periodically consult with the Secretary of Defense with respect to the establishment of rates of pay, in order to develop compatible or unified systems for basic pay. In addition, each agency shall consult with the Secretary of Defense on such other matters as the Secretary may deem appropriate in order to develop compatible or unified employment practices.93 Stat. 46322 U.S.C. 36511–202. The head of each agency shall, upon approval by the Secretary of Defense, adopt a schedule of basic pay pursuant to Section 1215 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) and adopt regulations governing other matters relating to pay and employment practices.93 Stat. 46522 U.S.C. 36551–203. The authority vested in the President by Section 1223(a) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 to coordinate the policies and activities of agencies (; ) is delegated to the Secretary of Defense. The Secretary shall exercise such functions in a manner which is in accord with the provisions of Sections 1–201 and 1–202 of this Order.93 Stat. 46722 U.S.C. 3663(a)

1–3.Panama Canal Commission1–301. The functions vested in the President and delegated to the Secretary of Defense in this Section 1–3 of this Order shall be carried out by the Secretary of Defense, who shall, in carrying out the said functions, provide, by redelegation or otherwise, for their performance, in a manner consistent with paragraph 3 of Article III of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977, by the Panama Canal Commission.1–302. The authority of the President under Section 1104 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) to fix the compensation of and to define the authorities and duties of the Deputy Administrator and the Chief Engineer is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 45722 U.S.C. 36141–303. The functions vested in the President by Sections 1418, 1801, and 2206 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (, 492, and 494; , 3811, and 3844) are delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 48722 U.S.C. 37781–304. The authority of the President under Section 1701 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) with respect to regulations applicable within the areas and installations made available to the United States pursuant to the Agreement in Implementation of Article III of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 49222 U.S.C. 38011–305. The function vested in the President by of the Panama Canal Code (; 6 P.C.C. 1281(b)), as amended, with respect to areas and installations in the Republic of Panama made available to the United States pursuant to the Agreement in Implementation of Article III of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 is delegated to the Secretary of Defense.Section 1281(b) of Title 676A Stat. 4551–306. The functions vested in the President by Sections 82 and 86 of Title 3 of the Panama Canal Code ( and 55; 3 P.C.C. 82 and 86), as amended, are delegated to the Secretary of Defense.76A Stat. 54[1–307. Rescinded by Ex. Ord. No. 12652, , 53 F.R. 36775, eff. .]Sept. 19, 1988Jan. 1, 19891–308. Except to the extent heretofore delegated, the functions vested in the President pursuant to subchapter II of Chapter 2 of Title I of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 () [ et seq.] are hereby delegated to the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 46322 U.S.C. 3651

1–4.Other Agencies1–401. The functions vested in the President by Sections 1111 and 3301 of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 ( and 497; and 3871), are delegated to the Secretary of State. The Secretary shall perform these functions in coordination with the Secretary of Defense.93 Stat. 45922 U.S.C. 36211–402. The functions vested in the President by Sections 1112(d), 1344(b), and 1504(b) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (, 484, and 488; , 3754(b), and 3784(b)) are delegated to the Secretary of State.93 Stat. 46022 U.S.C. 3622(d)1–403. The functions vested in the President by Section 1243(a)(1) of the Panama Canal Act of 1979 (; ) are delegated to the Director of the Office of Personnel Management.93 Stat. 47322 U.S.C. 3681(a)(1)1–404. Paragraphs (22) and (23) of Section 1 of Executive Order No. 11609, as amended [set out as a note under , The President], and Executive Order No. 11713 are revoked.section 301 of Title 3Jimmy Carter.
